title: Developer Integration Checklist
summary: A concise checklist for teams deciding whether a workload belongs on Arcium and what they need to understand before turning privacy requirements into an implementation plan.

bodySections.title: Define the sensitive inputs
bodySections.body: Start by naming the exact inputs or state transitions that should not be exposed. If that part is still fuzzy, the architecture conversation is premature.

bodySections.title: Define the visible outputs
bodySections.body: Confidential systems still need to produce useful application behavior. Be explicit about what users, counterparties, or other contracts must learn when the workload finishes.

bodySections.title: Evaluate the user experience tradeoff
bodySections.body: Privacy has a product cost if it introduces unacceptable latency, complexity, or trust friction. A good integration plan treats those tradeoffs as first-class constraints.
